2606.07017.title: The Sim-to-Real Gap of Foundation Model Agents: A Unified MDP Perspective
2606.07017.paper_summary: Foundation model agents are increasingly deployed for real-world decision-making, but suffer from the sim-to-real gap. While robotics and classical control have mature frameworks to address this gap, the foundation model community is treating agent robustness as an entirely novel phenomenon. Our paper proposes formalizing the foundation model agent evaluation and training gap as a classical sim-to-real problem structured entirely around the four elements of a Markov Decision Process, including Observation, Action, Transition, and Reward. In this paper, we set a comprehensive research agenda that translates classical discrepancies into the foundation model domain and advocates for adopting established solutions like domain randomization. We provide concrete examples, such as a multilingual tool calling to demonstrate how severe observation space gaps lead to operationally invalid actions despite correct semantic intent. Ultimately, this agenda aims to drive a paradigm shift, yielding a unified vocabulary and standardized stress test benchmarks to foster a new generation of highly trustworthy agents for reliable real-world applications.
2606.07017.paper_summary_zh: 基礎模型代理人越來越多地被用於現實世界的決策，但遭遇了模擬與現實之間的差距。雖然機器人技術和傳統控制擁有成熟的框架來解決這一差距，但基礎模型社群卻將代理人的穩健性視為一種全新的現象。我們的論文提議將基礎模型代理人的評估和訓練差距形式化為一個完全圍繞馬可夫決策過程的四個元素結構的傳統模擬到現實問題，包括觀察、行動、轉換和獎勵。在這篇論文中，我們設定了一個全面的研究議程，將傳統的差異轉化為基礎模型領域，並倡導採用已建立的解決方案，如領域隨機化。我們提供了具體的例子，例如一個多語言工具的調用，以展示如何嚴重的觀察空間差距導致操作上無效的行動，儘管語義意圖正確。最終，這一議程旨在推動範式轉變，產生統一的詞彙和標準化的壓力測試基準，以促進新一代高度可信的代理人，實現可靠的現實世界應用。
2606.07017.author: Xiaoou Liu et.al.
2606.07017.authors: Xiaoou Liu, Tiejin Chen, Weibo Li, Xiyang Hu, Hua Wei
